title: The First 90 Days: Updated and Expanded
subtitle: Proven Strategies for Getting Up to Speed Faster and Smarter
author: Michael D. Watkins
description: The definitive transition manual for new leaders at every level — a structured framework for diagnosing situations, building alliances, and accelerating performance in the most critical period of any new role.
keyIdeas: The Three Phases: Prepare, Manage, Accelerate
Diagnosing the Business: seven dimensions to assess
Negotiating Success: aligning expectations with your boss
The STARS Model: Startup, Turnaround, Accelerated Growth, Realignment, Sustaining Success
Building Personal Credibility: the FAST model
Building Your Team: assessing, restructuring, and engaging
Securing Early Wins: what to do in the first 60 days
Accelerating Your Learning: the learning plan as a core tool
keyTakeaways: The first 90 days determine the trajectory of your entire tenure — invest as much attention in the transition as the role itself.
STARS models shape strategy: a turnaround is fundamentally different from a startup — diagnose your situation before acting.
Your first conversation with your new boss should be a structured negotiation, not a casual check-in.
90% of new leaders waste the early weeks on low-priority activities instead of doing the strategic primary research that the role requires.
Self-orientation erodes trust faster than any mistake; diagnose and consciously manage whether people see you as for or against them.
Early wins must be visible, meaningful, and aligned with what your stakeholders value — not just what you enjoy doing.
Building a high-performing team is often the highest-leverage first move — assess current talent first, then make changes by 6-8 weeks.
Learning faster than anyone expects is itself a competitive advantage — write a formal learning plan and share it with your boss.
whoShouldRead: Newly promoted managers or executives moving into a bigger role
Career changers transitioning to a new industry or function
Leaders taking over a struggling team, division, or company
Professionals joining a new company at mid-to-senior level
Entrepreneurs navigating the transition from founder/operator to CEO
Anyone who wants to reduce the friction and failure rate of transitions
